ops.title: Never promote an implementation artifact into a hard scope filter — the user wants globally optimal decisions; constraints must be user-stated or structural
ops.body:
2026-07-17, cc-steer C4: I deferred the ~$50 base-model sweep because athome's `BASE_MODELS` marked only qwen3-8b `serves_locally=True`, reasoning "a base that can't serve can't be the watcher". The user: "stop doing random artificial constraints like this, just do the best globally optimal thing, no one said it _has_ to run locally."

**Why:** Local MLX serving was the *current implementation's* deployment posture, not a stated requirement. Promoting that artifact into an admission filter silently shrank the decision space (3 arms → 1) and deferred the whole experiment on a condition nobody set.

**How to apply:** Before filtering scope or deferring work on a constraint, classify it. User-stated, plan-stated, or structurally unavoidable → honor it. An artifact of the current implementation, or an assumption I introduced → treat it as a tradeoff/metadata dimension, optimize globally, and surface the tradeoff in the result instead of pre-deciding with it. Related: [[dont-degrade-match-the-strong-path]].
